title: polypodium vulgare subsp. cambricum
intro: Polypodium vulgare subsp. cambricum, also known as Welsh polypody, is a type of fern native to the UK, Ireland, and parts of Europe, characterized by its large, leathery fronds and distinctive brown or black spores. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, burns, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The medicinal properties of Welsh polypody have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, or applied topically as a poultice or ointment to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
